description:
You are acting as a newly-hired backup engineer who must set up password-protected
    SSH access for your automated integrity-checking scripts.  
    The senior engineer has asked you to do three things and leave verifiable proof
    that everything was done correctly.

    1. Generate a brand-new 4096-bit RSA key-pair *with a pass-phrase* and store it
       under the directory  
       /home/user/backup_verification/keys/  
       The private key **must** be called “backup_id_rsa” and the public key
       “backup_id_rsa.pub”.

    2. Append the *full* public-key line (the single line that starts with  
       “ssh-rsa …”) to the simulated remote host’s file  
       /home/user/remote_host/.ssh/authorized_keys  
       (create the directory structure if it does not already exist).
       • Make sure you do **not** create a duplicate entry if the key is already
         present.  
       • Do **not** remove the comment line that is already inside that file.

    3. Produce the log file /home/user/backup_verification/ssh_setup.log  
       containing **exactly three lines** in the precise order shown below.  
       The automated test will fail if the format differs in any way:

            TIMESTAMP:<UTC-ISO8601>  
            PUBKEY_FINGERPRINT:<fingerprint as printed by “ssh-keygen -lf …/backup_id_rsa.pub”>  
            AUTHORIZED_KEYS_CHECK:OK  

       • <UTC-ISO8601> must be the current UTC time in the form
         YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ (for example 2024-01-19T08:43:12Z).  
       • The fingerprint string must be copied verbatim exactly as it appears in
         the output of `ssh-keygen -lf /home/user/backup_verification/keys/backup_id_rsa.pub`
         (including the “SHA256:” prefix or the MD5 hash and its bits counter).  
       • The third line must literally contain the string “AUTHORIZED_KEYS_CHECK:OK”
         only if you confirmed that the public key is present *once and only once*
         in /home/user/remote_host/.ssh/authorized_keys.  
         If you find any mismatch or duplication, write
         “AUTHORIZED_KEYS_CHECK:FAIL” instead.

    Additional requirements & hints:
      • The key-pair must be created with a pass-phrase of your choosing; do **not**
        generate an unprotected key.  
      • Make sure the private key file is readable only by you
        (i.e. permission 600).  
      • The directory /home/user/backup_verification already exists and is writable
        by you, but the keys/ sub-directory may have to be created.  
      • The test harness will check that:
          – both key files exist in the correct location,  
          – the public key line is present exactly once in
            /home/user/remote_host/.ssh/authorized_keys,  
          – ssh_setup.log exists with the three lines formatted exactly as
            specified,  
          – the fingerprint in ssh_setup.log matches the key on disk,  
          – backup_id_rsa is not world-readable.

    Do not output the commands you plan to run here—only use the terminal
    to perform the steps so that the required files are created exactly as
    specified by the senior engineer.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth used by the automated tests.
    The agent must not see anything inside this tag.
    -->

    <!-- Directory that already exists and is writable -->
    <pre_existing_dir>/home/user/backup_verification</pre_existing_dir>

    <!-- Simulated remote host directory that the agent may have to create -->
    <remote_host_path>/home/user/remote_host/.ssh</remote_host_path>

    <!-- Initial contents of the simulated remote host authorized_keys file -->
    <authorized_keys_initial_content>
        # remote host authorized keys
    </authorized_keys_initial_content>

    <!-- Expected artefacts AFTER the agent finishes -->
    <expected_private_key>/home/user/backup_verification/keys/backup_id_rsa</expected_private_key>
    <expected_public_key>/home/user/backup_verification/keys/backup_id_rsa.pub</expected_public_key>

    <!-- Permissions that will be validated -->
    <private_key_permissions>600</private_key_permissions>

    <!-- Log file path and mandatory lines pattern -->
    <log_file>/home/user/backup_verification/ssh_setup.log</log_file>
    <log_line_1_prefix>TIMESTAMP:</log_line_1_prefix>
    <log_line_2_prefix>PUBKEY_FINGERPRINT:</log_line_2_prefix>
    <log_line_3_ok>AUTHORIZED_KEYS_CHECK:OK</log_line_3_ok>
    <log_line_3_fail>AUTHORIZED_KEYS_CHECK:FAIL</log_line_3_fail>

    <!-- Key presence test -->
    <public_key_must_appear_once>true</public_key_must_appear_once>
name: task_000000_51308809
